Title: Accumulation of the RNA polymerase subunit RpoB depends on RNA editing by OsPPR16 and affects chloroplast development during early leaf development in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLS-DYW pentatricopeptide repeat (PPR) protein with 14 PPR motifs, DYW domain, chloroplast transit peptide
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RNAi knockdown; complementation with native promoter; RNA editing assay by RT-PCR/cDNA sequencing; chlorophyll measurement; TEM; Western blot; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for chloroplast development and chlorophyll accumulation during early leaf development by enabling editing of plastid rpoB transcripts; loss causes pale early leaves and delayed greening
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and chloroplast function
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in young leaves during early chloroplast development (inferred from Fig. S8 and discussion)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early leaf developmental stage; de-etiolation after dark growth; norflurazon treatment for gun phenotype assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPPR16 is a chloroplast-localized PLS-DYW PPR protein associated with plastid nucleoids. 2. OsPPR16 is specifically required for RNA editing of chloroplast rpoB-545. 3. Loss of OsPPR16 reduces RpoB accumulation, lowers PEP-dependent transcription, severely decreases tRNAGlu accumulation, and limits chlorophyll biosynthesis during early leaf development.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes pale early leaves, delayed greening, impaired chloroplast development, and reduced early chlorophyll accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts post-transcriptionally via RNA editing rather than direct catalysis in chlorophyll biosynthesis; major effect is developmental-stage specific and largely recovers by later leaf stages
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsPPR16 promoter used for complementation (indigenous/native)
